results.rendered_html: vi-loma mf(ā)n. (fr. 3. vi + loman) against the hair or grain, turned the wrong way, inverted, contrary to the usual or proper course, opposed (pavana-vil˚, ‘turned against the wind’; am ind., ‘backwards’),  GopBr. ;  Var. ;  Rājat.

results.rendered_html:
viloma (2. vi + loman)
1〉 adj. (f. ā) = pratīpa  H. 1465 . an. 3,472 .  MED. m. 53 .
a〉 wider das Haar —, wider den Strich —, in entgegengesetzter Richtung gehend, in verkehrter Ordnung laufend: trikavalanavilomaṃ vīkṣya dūrātsvadeśam so v. a. hinter sich  RĀJA-TAR. 1,374 .
— b〉 widerspänstig: ein Elephant  VARĀH. BṚH. S. 94,12 .
— 2〉 m.
a〉 Schlange.
— b〉 Hund.
— c〉 Varuṇa  H. an.   MED. 
— 3〉 f. ī Myrobalanenbaum  H. an.   MED. 
— 4〉 n. = araghaṭṭaka  H. an.   MED.  (hier fälschlich arapaṭṭana).

results.rendered_html:
viloma a. (mī f.)
1 Inverted, reverse, inverse, contrary, opposite.
2 Produced in the reverse order.
3 Backward.
— maḥ
1 Reverse order, inversion.
2 A dog.
3 A snake.
4 N. of Varuṇa.
— maṃ A waterwheel, machine for raising water from a well.
Comp.
— utpanna, -ja, -jāta,
— varṇa a. ‘born in the reverse order’; i. e. born of a mother whose caste is superior to the father's; cf. pratilomaja also.
— kriyā, -vidhiḥ
1 a reverse action.
2 a rule of inversion (in math.).
— jihvaḥ an elephant.
